title: After yearly evaluation raise offer could be better. write a letter to employer not agreeing to raise offer. include inf…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: After yearly evaluation raise offer could be better. write a letter to employer not agreeing to raise offer. include information that I'm hired as process technician, but performing tasks of maintenance technician, programmer, designer. add that I'm spearheading 3d print in the department, designing and printing needed parts and helping with 3d printing . include information that I'm developing good relationships with support engineers from ASM, MAXEM, QUIPTECH, IPP that helps get needed support for the machine much quicker. write that i need raise because i want to buy a house.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
[Your Name]
[Your Address]
[City, State, ZIP Code]
[Email Address]
[Phone Number]
[Date]

[Employer's Name]
[Company Name]
[Company Address]
[City, State, ZIP Code]

Dear [Employer's Name],

I hope this letter finds you well. I am writing to express my concerns about the recent raise offer I received after my yearly evaluation. While I appreciate the recognition of my hard work in our company, I must state that I believe the raise offered does not accurately reflect the additional responsibilities and contributions I have made since being hired as a process technician.

Throughout my time with the company, I have not only performed the tasks of a process technician but have also taken on the roles of a maintenance technician, programmer, and designer. This has allowed me to diversify my skills and help the company in various capacities, ultimately leading to a more efficient and productive work environment. 

Additionally, I have spearheaded the integration of 3D printing within our department, designing and printing parts as needed as well as providing guidance and assistance with 3D printing technology. This has proved to be a valuable asset to our department, cutting both cost and time in obtaining the necessary parts required for our operations.

Moreover, my efforts in cultivating relationships with support engineers from leading industry companies like ASM, MAXEM, QUIPTECH, and IPP have resulted in faster support response times and improved machine performance. The relationships I've built with these key figures have greatly contributed to the teamwork and overall success of our department.

These extra responsibilities and accomplishments have had a significant positive impact on the overall efficiency and performance of our department, and I believe they warrant a more substantial raise. 

I would also like to take this opportunity to share that I am currently looking into purchasing a house. An adequate raise would provide me with the financial security I need to move forward with this significant life milestone. 

I understand that the company must consider several factors when offering salary raises, but I am confident that my ongoing dedication and valuable contributions to the team justify a higher raise. I kindly request the opportunity to discuss this matter further in a meeting at your earliest convenience.

Thank you for your time and understanding. I look forward to our discussion and am optimistic that we can reach a mutually beneficial agreement.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]

turns.text:
[Your Name]
[Your Address]
[City, State, ZIP Code]
[Email Address]
[Phone Number]
[Date]

[Employer's Name]
[Company Name]
[Company Address]
[City, State, ZIP Code]

Dear [Employer's Name],

I hope this letter finds you well. I am writing to express my concerns regarding the recent raise offer received after my yearly evaluation. While I appreciate that my hard work and dedication to the company has been recognized, I must convey that the proposed raise does not adequately reflect the numerous additional responsibilities and contributions that I have made since my hiring as a process technician.

In my time at the company, not only have I expertly executed the tasks of a process technician, but I have also assumed the roles of a maintenance technician, programmer, IT support, and designer. These added responsibilities have enabled me to vastly broaden my skillset and provide extensive support to my colleagues and the company as a whole.

Furthermore, I have taken the initiative to spearhead the introduction of 3D printing within the department, designing and printing necessary parts, as well as assisting engineers with 3D printing technology. This invaluable resource has significantly reduced costs and production times, greatly benefiting the operation of our department.

In addition, my ongoing efforts in forging strong relationships with support engineers from leading industry companies such as ASM, MAXEM, QUIPTECH, and IPP have resulted in speedier machine support and improved efficiency. The bonds I have cultivated with these key individuals have had a profound impact on our department's teamwork and overall success.

My extensive contributions and commitment to the success of our department warrant a more significant raise than the one currently proposed. I also want to share with you that I am planning to buy a house soon, a decision influenced by the fact that not long ago I was facing potential homelessness or a return to Poland. An appropriate raise is crucial to ensuring my financial stability and securing this important life milestone.

I understand that the company must take several factors into account when determining salary raises. However, I am confident that my ongoing dedication and meaningful contributions to the team justify a more substantial increase. I kindly request the opportunity to discuss this matter further in a meeting at your earliest convenience.

Thank you for your time and understanding. I look forward to our discussion and trust that we can reach a beneficial agreement for both parties.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
